era_context: Middle Bronze Age. The Hyksos consolidate their rule in Egypt; Kassite power rises in Mesopotamia; Minoan civilization flourishes on Crete; the Indus Valley Civilization continues in its mature phase. In China, the Xia or early Shang period is underway.
disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed. No specific events are known for this year.
historiographic_note: This year has no documented specific events in any surviving historical source. Like -1652, this year is historically invisible in the written record. Our understanding of the period comes entirely from archaeological evidence and later historical syntheses. The absence of recorded events is not unusual for the Bronze Age; most individual years leave no trace in history unless they witnessed major political upheaval, military conflict, or cultural innovation that was recorded or left substantial material remains.
